context: M1 proves the rights-gate end-to-end on a real LoC source. This task runs one approved high-PD source (Chronicling America is the primary candidate) through the full pipeline: discovery, polite fetch, rights-gate, cultural-sensitivity screen, and provenance-manifest generation. Every item not cleared must be logged in the quarantine ledger. Depends on client-001 and ci-001. See PLAN.md (M1 exit criteria).
acceptanceCriteria: One approved high-PD source (e.g. Chronicling America) is selected and documented with rationale for its approval.
The full pipeline (discovery, polite fetch, rights-gate, cultural-sensitivity screen, manifest) runs end-to-end without manual intervention.
100% of cleared items carry a complete frozen provenance manifest; items without one are excluded from the cleared set.
All access remained within loc-policy.yml limits; cache was used for re-fetches; zero policy breaches occurred.
Every ineligible and needs-review item is recorded in the quarantine ledger (ledger/quarantine.jsonl) with a reason code.
The cleared corpus passes the CI provenance-completeness gate and rights-gate golden tests.
output: A committed cleared corpus dataset with frozen manifests and a populated quarantine ledger, passing CI gates.
title: Run an approved high-PD source (e.g. Chronicling America) through the gate to produce cleared corpus + manifests
objective: Run one approved high-PD LoC source end-to-end through the full pipeline, producing a cleared corpus where every item carries a complete frozen manifest and every non-cleared item is recorded in the quarantine ledger with a reason.
